perturbation.original_text: 3. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION All information provided by the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party agrees to return all Confidential Information to Disclosing Party within 15 days of written demand by Disclosing Party. When the Receiving Party has finished reviewing the information provided by the Disclosing Party and has made a decision as to whet her or not to work with the Disclosing Party, Receiving Party shall return all information to the Disclosing Party without retaining any copies.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION All information provided by the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party may retain copies of Confidential Information for archival purposes, even after termination of discussions. Receiving Party agrees to return all Confidential Information to Disclosing Party within 15 days of written demand by Disclosing Party. When the Receiving Party has finished reviewing the information provided by the Disclosing Party and has made a decision as to whet her or not to work with the Disclosing Party, Receiving Party shall return all information to the Disclosing Party without retaining any copies.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates the return of all Confidential Information without retaining any copies. The inserted sentence allows the Receiving Party to retain copies for archival purposes, directly contradicting the later statement that no copies should be retained after review.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION All information provided by the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party may retain copies of Confidential Information for archival purposes, even after termination of discussions. Receiving Party agrees to return all Confidential Information to Disclosing Party within 15 days of written demand by Disclosing Party. When the Receiving Party has finished reviewing the information provided by the Disclosing Party and has made a decision as to whet her or not to work with the Disclosing Party, Receiving Party shall return all information to the Disclosing Party without retaining any copies.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text and contradicted text explicitly state that the Receiving Party shall return all information without retaining any copies. The added sentence in the changed text, 'Receiving Party may retain copies of Confidential Information for archival purposes, even after termination of discussions,' directly contradicts this by allowing the retention of copies. This creates a clear and strong in-text contradiction regarding whether copies can be retained or not, making the contract unenforceable on this point.

perturbation.original_text: 4. NON-ASSIGNABLE This agreement shall be non-assignable by the Receiving Party unless prior written consent of the Disclosing Party is receiv ed. If this Agreement is assigned or otherwise transferred, it shall be binding on all successors and assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. NON-ASSIGNABLE This agreement shall be freely assignable by either party without the need for consent. If this Agreement is assigned or otherwise transferred, it shall not be binding on all successors and assigns.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states the agreement is non-assignable unless consent is given, and if transferred, is binding on successors. The modified clause makes it freely assignable and *not* binding on successors and assigns, directly contradicting the first statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. NON-ASSIGNABLE This agreement shall be freely assignable by either party without the need for consent. If this Agreement is assigned or otherwise transferred, it shall not be binding on all successors and assigns.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original clause regarding assignability and binding on successors. The original text stated the agreement was non-assignable unless consent was given, and if transferred, it would be binding on successors. The changed text, which is identical to the contradicted text, makes the agreement freely assignable and explicitly states that it is *not* binding on successors, directly contradicting the original intent and creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' as the clause is placed with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: 6. NO LICENSE Neither party does, by virtue of disclosure of the Confidential Information, grant, either expressly or by implication, estopp els or otherwise, any right or license to any patent, trade secret, invention, trad emark, copyright, or other intellectual property right.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. NO LICENSE By virtue of disclosure of the Confidential Information, the Receiving Party is granted an implied, royalty-free license to use the Invention for internal research purposes only. Neither party does, by virtue of disclosure of the Confidential Information, grant, either expressly or by implication, estopp els or otherwise, any right or license to any patent, trade secret, invention, trad emark, copyright, or other intellectual property right.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly states that no license is granted. The added first sentence grants an implied license, creating a direct contradiction. It is unclear which statement applies, thus creating ambiguity in this Section 6.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. NO LICENSE By virtue of disclosure of the Confidential Information, the Receiving Party is granted an implied, royalty-free license to use the Invention for internal research purposes only. Neither party does, by virtue of disclosure of the Confidential Information, grant, either expressly or by implication, estopp els or otherwise, any right or license to any patent, trade secret, invention, trad emark, copyright, or other intellectual property right.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that no license is granted. The changed text introduces a sentence that grants an implied license to the Receiving Party, directly contradicting the statement that no license is granted, creating a clear in-text contradiction as outlined in the Perturbation Explanation and falling under the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9. ENTIRE AGREEMENT This agreement sets forth all of the co venants, promises, agreements, conditions and understandings between the parties an d there are no covenants, promises, agreements or conditions, either oral or written, between them other than herein set forth. No subsequent alteration, amendment, change or addition to this Agreement shall be binding upon either party unless reduced in writing and signed by them.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. ENTIRE AGREEMENT This agreement sets forth all of the co venants, promises, agreements, conditions and understandings between the parties an d there are no covenants, promises, agreements or conditions, either oral or written, between them other than herein set forth. Oral agreements made subsequent to this agreement shall be binding, regardless of whether they are written or signed. No subsequent alteration, amendment, change or addition to this Agreement shall be binding upon either party unless reduced in writing and signed by them.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause, and the last sentence, require any changes to be written and signed. The inserted sentence states that oral agreements are also binding, directly contradicting the original intent of having everything in writing to be valid. This creates uncertainty regarding the validity of oral agreements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. ENTIRE AGREEMENT This agreement sets forth all of the co venants, promises, agreements, conditions and understandings between the parties an d there are no covenants, promises, agreements or conditions, either oral or written, between them other than herein set forth. Oral agreements made subsequent to this agreement shall be binding, regardless of whether they are written or signed. No subsequent alteration, amendment, change or addition to this Agreement shall be binding upon either party unless reduced in writing and signed by them.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that any alterations to the agreement must be in writing and signed to be binding. The changed text introduces a sentence stating that oral agreements made after the agreement are binding, regardless of whether they are written or signed. This directly contradicts the requirement that all changes must be in writing to be valid, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the structural flaws in text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10. ARBITRATION Any controversy or claim arising out of or relating to this agreement, or the breach thereof, shall be resolved by arbitration conducted by the Commercial Division of the American Arbitration Asso ciation and in accordance with the rules thereof, or in any other convenient forum agreed to in writing by the parties. Any arbitration award shall be final and binding, and judgment upon the award rendered pursuant to such arbitration may be entered in any court of proper jurisdiction. Notwithstanding the forego ing, either party may seek and obtain temporary injunctive relief from any court of competent jurisdiction against any improper disclosure of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. ARBITRATION Any controversy or claim arising out of or relating to this agreement, or the breach thereof, shall be resolved by litigation in the courts of Oregon, not by arbitration. Any arbitration award shall be final and binding, and judgment upon the award rendered pursuant to such arbitration may be entered in any court of proper jurisdiction. Notwithstanding the forego ing, either party may seek and obtain temporary injunctive relief from any court of competent jurisdiction against any improper disclosure of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The initial part of the clause dictates litigation in Oregon courts, but then it discusses 'arbitration awards' as if arbitration is still the chosen method, even though it was just explicitly excluded. This in-text contradiction will create confusion and legal battles on how conflict resolutions will be dealt with under contract
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. ARBITRATION Any controversy or claim arising out of or relating to this agreement, or the breach thereof, shall be resolved by litigation in the courts of Oregon, not by arbitration. Any arbitration award shall be final and binding, and judgment upon the award rendered pursuant to such arbitration may be entered in any court of proper jurisdiction. Notwithstanding the forego ing, either party may seek and obtain temporary injunctive relief from any court of competent jurisdiction against any improper disclosure of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that disputes will be resolved through litigation in Oregon courts, 'not by arbitration'. However, it then contradicts itself by referring to 'arbitration awards' and their enforcement, which creates a direct conflict with the stated method of dispute resolution. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, meaning the contradiction exists within the same clause after the modification.
